title: Welcome to On Guard
text: On Guard is a baseball data science blog built on a Neo4j graph of Statcast data. The goal is to explore pitch and swing mechanics, player similarity, and situational tendencies — with a particular focus on the Cleveland Guardians.

title: Welcome to On Guard
text: On Guard is a baseball data science blog built on a Neo4j graph of Statcast data. The goal is to explore pitch and swing mechanics, player similarity, and situational tendencies — with a particular focus on the Cleveland Guardians.

title: Welcome to On Guard
text:
The stack
Analysis is driven by a graph where every pitch is a node connected to:

The pitcher who threw it
The batter who faced it
Its pitch mechanics cluster (velocity, movement, spin)
Its swing mechanics cluster (bat speed, attack angle, swing length)
The zone it landed in
The contact outcome if it was put in play

Node embeddings are generated via FastRP in Neo4j GDS, capturing each player’s structural fingerprint across the season. Predictive models use XGBoost on top of those embeddings in a staged pipeline.

title: Welcome to On Guard
text:
Coming up

Swing archetype breakdown for the 2025 Guardians roster
Pitch cluster analysis — what makes José Ramírez hard to pitch to
Batter similarity: who swings like whom

Stay tuned.

title: About
text:
On Guard is a baseball data science blog with a bias toward coverage of the Cleveland Guardians.
Analysis is built on a Statcast graph database using Neo4j, with clustering, graph embeddings, and staged predictive models. The underlying tooling lives in the neo4j_baseball repo.
Topics covered:

Swing mechanics clustering and archetypes
Pitch mechanics and sequencing
Graph-based player similarity
Guardians roster analysis
